metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document and enforce that the receiver's config must have `verify.enable_code_review = true` for `produce_code_review_verdict` to run (otherwise `_inert_code_review_verdict()` silently returns `verdict: 'PASS'` → `+MAX`); either treat `coverage.enabled=False` in the adapter as a `coverage_gap=True` / BLOCK path, or add a startup assertion that fails fast when `[gerrit_voter].enabled=true` but `verify.enable_code_review=false`.
findings.finding: The adapter wraps `produce_code_review_verdict`, which is gated by `verify.enable_code_review` (defaulting to `false` — confirmed at gate_dispatch.py:355-363 and config.py:284). When that flag is false, `produce_code_review_verdict` returns `verdict: 'PASS'` with `coverage.enabled=False` (the inert verdict from `_inert_code_review_verdict()`). The adapter then maps PASS→+MAX, silently granting every change a green vote with zero LLM work performed. The plan specifies only the `[gerrit_voter]` receiver-level config gate but never mentions that `verify.enable_code_review` must also be `true` in the receiver's config for the gate to actually run — creating a silent fail-open that directly contradicts the plan's otherwise pervasive fail-closed posture.
findings.location: "The adapter seam" section; AC1; Staged opt-in section
findings.suggested_fix: In the adapter, after calling `produce_code_review_verdict`, check `verdict.get('coverage', {}).get('enabled') is False` and treat it as a coverage-gap BLOCK (→ -1 + coverage-gap robot comment), not a PASS. Document this as the required config invariant in the [gerrit_voter] config schema section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the adapter handles the inert/disabled-path verdict from `produce_code_review_verdict` (when `coverage.enabled=False`): either treat it as a special third outcome (no-vote / skip), map it to a specific label value, or require that the voter's `[gerrit_voter]` config activation implies the gate is enabled at the repo level.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify what the adapter (`code_review_decision`) should do when `produce_code_review_verdict` returns the INERT disabled-path verdict (verdict='PASS', coverage.enabled=False). In the Gerrit voter context, `verify.enable_code_review` is a per-repo capability flag distinct from the `[gerrit_voter]` config; if the gate is disabled at the repo level, `produce_code_review_verdict` returns a PASS verdict that was never actually reviewed. The adapter would map this to `decision=PASS, label_value=+MAX`, potentially approving an unreviewed change with a legitimate +MAX vote — an unspecified fallback for an incomplete sub-step.
findings.location: ## The adapter seam (the d251-S4b contract) / ## Staged opt-in + rollback

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the robot-comment vocabulary will use Gerrit's formal `robot_comments` field in `ReviewInput` (requiring `robot_id`/`robot_run_id` and the service account's `robotComments` permission) or a plain `message` string, and confirm that whichever path is chosen is provisioned/supported by the d251 service-account setup.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'robot comment' posting without specifying whether it uses Gerrit's formal `robot_comments` field in ReviewInput vs. a plain `message` field — these are distinct Gerrit REST API surfaces with different permission requirements, payload shapes, and UI treatment (robot comments require `robot_id`/`robot_run_id`, appear in a separate UI tab, and may require the service account to be declared a robot account in Gerrit config).
findings.location: Robot-comment vocabulary section; AC5 ('the robot comment distinguishes…'); REST vote failure behavior section
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the robot-comment vocabulary section: 'The vote POST uses `ReviewInput.message` (plain string) rather than `ReviewInput.robot_comments[]`, so no `robot_id`/`robot_run_id` is required and no additional Gerrit robotComments ACL is needed.' OR 'The vote POST uses `ReviewInput.robot_comments[]` with `robot_id="rebar-llm-review"` and `robot_run_id=<change_id>/<patchset>`; d251 must provision the service account with the robotComments permission.' Pick one and make it explicit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the robot comment is submitted as a `robot_comments` map entry (Gerrit's structured RobotCommentInput with `robotId`/`robotRunId`/`message`) or as the `message` cover-message field in SetReview, and document the chosen field in the REST client spec so the stub test validates the correct payload shape.
findings.finding: The plan's robot-comment vocabulary is specified as a plain first-line string tag, but Gerrit's SetReview API requires robot comments to be submitted as a structured `robot_comments` map (with `robotId`, `robotRunId`, `line`, `message` fields per the Gerrit REST API), not as a free-form message field. Treating robot comments as a plain message string would cause the Gerrit server to reject or ignore them, or route them to a human `comments` field instead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which Gerrit REST endpoint the patchset handler uses to fetch the change diff (e.g., `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch?download` or `/files` + `/diff`), the expected format passed to `produce_code_review_verdict`, and confirm the same Basic Auth credential is used for both the diff GET and the vote POST.
findings.finding: The patchset-created handler is required to 'assemble the change diff' (AC 3), but the plan does not specify which Gerrit REST endpoint to call, what diff format to request, or how authentication is applied for this GET request (as opposed to the vote POST). An implementing agent must choose among Gerrit's several diff endpoints (/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch, /files/{path}/diff, etc.) with no guidance.
findings.location: AC 3 / 'What' section / REST vote failure behavior section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Diff-fetch' subsection (or a note in the REST client section) naming the endpoint, e.g.: 'Use `GET /changes/{change-id}/revisions/{revision-id}/patch` (base64-encoded unified diff, decoded before passing as `diff_text`); the same Basic Auth credential as the vote POST.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the adapter implementation (or AC5) the exact boolean logic for `coverage_gap`: `coverage_gap = verdict.get('coverage', {}).get('llm_unavailable', False) OR any(d.get('reason') == 'fail-closed-abstain' for d in verdict.get('coverage', {}).get('security_detectors', []))` — so a detector-abstain BLOCK (which has `verdict='BLOCK'`, not `'INDETERMINATE'`) is correctly tagged as coverage-gap.
findings.finding: The plan states `coverage_gap` is true when driven by `coverage.llm_unavailable` (INDETERMINATE path) OR any `coverage.security_detectors[].reason == 'fail-closed-abstain'` (a detector-abstain BLOCK). However, the BLOCK-from-detector-abstain verdict has `verdict='BLOCK'` (not INDETERMINATE) — confirmed by `detectors.py:119: verdict['verdict'] = 'BLOCK'` — so the adapter cannot distinguish a real-finding BLOCK from a fail-closed-abstain BLOCK by looking at `decision` alone. The boolean logic in the adapter to derive `coverage_gap=True` from the verdict dict is not specified, leaving a real risk that a detector-abstain BLOCK is mislabeled as `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — finding]` (a real finding) rather than `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap]` (infra failure), or vice versa.
findings.location: "Robot-comment vocabulary" section; AC5; "The adapter seam" section (return shape `coverage_gap: bool`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the adapter seam section: 'coverage_gap is True iff coverage.llm_unavailable=True (INDETERMINATE path) OR coverage.security_detectors contains any entry with reason=="fail-closed-abstain" (detector-abstain BLOCK path); the decision field alone is insufficient to derive coverage_gap because both BLOCK and INDETERMINATE can be coverage gaps.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific Gerrit REST endpoint used to cast the label vote (e.g., `POST /changes/{change-id}/revisions/{revision-id}/review` with a `ReviewInput` JSON body containing `labels` and `robot_comments`) so the stub can be written against the correct path.
findings.finding: The plan names no specific Gerrit REST endpoint for casting the label vote. The standard endpoint is POST /changes/{change-id}/revisions/{revision-id}/review (Gerrit's SetReview API), but without naming it the implementer must discover this independently, and an incorrect choice (e.g. the older SSH stream or /changes/{id}/vote) would produce a broken integration.
findings.location: REST vote failure behavior section; AC 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Vote REST call' subsection: 'POST /changes/{change-id}/revisions/{revision-id}/review with body {"labels": {"LLM-Review": <label_value>}, "robot_comments": {...}, "tag": "autogenerated:rebar"}; same Basic Auth credential as the diff-fetch GET.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the Gerrit auth credential type: specify whether the service account uses Gerrit HTTP Basic Auth (username + generated HTTP password), Bearer token, or another scheme, and confirm the server has the corresponding auth backend enabled — so the REST client's Authorization header is correct by construction.
findings.finding: The plan states auth as 'urllib + Basic Auth (service-account token provisioned by d251)' but Gerrit Basic Auth uses HTTP-password credentials (an account-specific generated password separate from the web-UI password), not a generic 'service-account token'. Whether d251 provisions an HTTP password, an OAuth Bearer token, or a Gerrit-specific API key is unspecified, and the credential type determines whether Basic Auth or a different Authorization header scheme is required.
findings.location: REST vote failure behavior section; Dependencies section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `coverage_gap` derivation rule to the adapter seam spec: define exactly which fields of the `produce_code_review_verdict` output set `coverage_gap=True` (i.e., `verdict['coverage'].get('llm_unavailable') is True` OR any entry in `verdict['coverage'].get('security_detectors', [])` has `reason == 'fail-closed-abstain'`).
findings.finding: The `coverage_gap: bool` field in the adapter output schema has no defined derivation rule: the plan specifies the output shape `{decision, label_value, coverage_gap, message}` but never states which fields of the `produce_code_review_verdict` result set `coverage_gap=True`. The robot-comment section mentions two distinct source conditions (`coverage.llm_unavailable` OR `coverage.security_detectors[].reason == 'fail-closed-abstain'`), but the adapter schema spec section does not define how to derive `coverage_gap` from those fields — the derivation protocol is only implied by the robot-comment vocabulary section, not co-located with the adapter schema definition.
findings.location: ## The adapter seam (the d251-S4b contract) / AC1 / ## Robot-comment vocabulary
findings.suggested_fix: In the adapter seam section, add: '`coverage_gap` is `True` iff the upstream verdict has `coverage.llm_unavailable == True` OR any entry in `coverage.security_detectors` has `reason == "fail-closed-abstain"`; it is `False` for a real-finding BLOCK (blocking findings present, neither condition holds).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which Gerrit REST endpoint the patchset handler uses to assemble `diff_text` (e.g., `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{id}/patch` base64-decoded, or per-file diff assembly) so the stub Gerrit in AC3 models the real response shape.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'a patchset-created handler assembles the change diff' but does not specify which Gerrit REST endpoint it uses to retrieve the diff text — Gerrit exposes at least three distinct surfaces (GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{id}/patch for a base64 patch, GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{id}/files + per-file diff, GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{id}/diff for a single file) with different response shapes, encoding, and size constraints. This is an unverified capability assertion because `diff_text: str` in the adapter seam implies a specific representation.
findings.location: AC3 ('assembles the change diff'); The adapter seam section ('diff_text: str')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the patchset-created handler description: 'Diff assembly uses `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{id}/patch` (base64-decoded) to produce the unified diff string passed as `diff_text`. The stub Gerrit returns a pre-encoded base64 patch fixture.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which Gerrit REST endpoint the patchset handler uses to fetch the change diff, the expected wire format (e.g., base64-encoded unified patch from `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch?download`, decoded before passing as `diff_text`), and confirm the same Basic Auth credential applies to both the diff GET and the vote POST.
findings.finding: The plan requires the patchset handler to 'assemble the change diff' (AC3) but names no Gerrit REST endpoint for fetching it. Gerrit offers at least three diff surfaces with incompatible formats: GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch (base64-encoded unified patch), GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/files/{path}/diff (per-file JSON diff), and GET /changes/{id}/patch (shorthand). The wrong choice produces a diff format incompatible with produce_code_review_verdict's diff_text parameter.
findings.location: AC 3 / 'What' section / REST vote failure behavior section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC 5 to say 'a test asserts all three tag variants' (PASS, BLOCK—finding, BLOCK—coverage-gap) to match the three-tag vocabulary defined in the robot-comment section.
findings.finding: The robot-comment vocabulary section defines THREE distinct tags (`[LLM-Review: PASS]`, `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — finding]`, `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap]`), but AC 5 asserts 'a test asserts both tags' — 'both' implies two. This cross-section inconsistency leaves it unclear whether the test must cover all three tags or only two, and which two.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'a test asserts both tags' in AC 5 with 'a test asserts all three tag variants: `[LLM-Review: PASS]`, `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — finding]`, and `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap]`, covering advisory-findings-kept-at-+MAX, real-finding -1, and fail-closed -1 respectively.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit within-story sequencing plan (thin vertical slice first): step 1 — adapter + vote-mapping tests (ACs 1–2, seam pinned); step 2 — patchset handler + stub-Gerrit end-to-end (AC 3); step 3 — config gate (AC 4); step 4 — robot-comment vocabulary (AC 5); step 5 — REST failure behavior (AC 6); step 6 — ADR (AC 7). Each step produces a reviewable increment before the next begins.
findings.finding: The leaf ticket carries 7 ACs spanning a new package with ≥4 distinct components (adapter, REST client, patchset handler, config schema + allowlist logic, robot-comment vocabulary, ADR) and ≥3 layers (domain adapter, REST transport, config/policy). No within-story thin-vertical-slice sequencing is specified — all components are built in one horizontal wave with no evidence-gate between them, making it difficult to execute or review coherently in a single session and increasing the risk that a late-discovered seam issue (e.g., the REST client's retry contract conflicting with the stub's expectations) invalidates earlier work.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all 7 ACs) / What / Scope sections
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's 'What' or a new 'Sequencing' section, enumerate the 5–6 implementation steps above with explicit completion gates (e.g., 'ACs 1–2 tests green before REST client work begins'), and consider whether ACs 5–6 (robot comments + REST failure) are stable enough to split into a follow-on leaf if the core adapter+handler slice proves large enough to validate the seam.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'ADR 0013 (Gerrit voter architecture)' to the 'What' and 'Scope - IN' sections so the documentation artifact is an explicitly scoped deliverable alongside the code.
findings.finding: ADR 0013 is listed as a deliverable only in AC7 but is absent from the 'What' and 'Scope' sections of the plan body, making it an implicit rather than explicitly scoped output — a reviewer could reasonably scope it out as 'just a test artifact' rather than a first-class deliverable.
findings.suggested_fix: In '## What', append: '- `docs/adr/0013-gerrit-llm-review-voter.md` — ADR capturing the code_review_decision seam contract, vote-mapping (incl. INDETERMINATE fail-closed), staged opt-in/rollback config, robot-comment vocabulary, and REST-failure posture.' In '## Scope - IN', append: '+ ADR 0013'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit deploy-ordering note to the Staged opt-in section (or the Dependencies section) stating that `enabled=true` must not be set until d251's LLM-Review label, submit requirement, and service-account provisioning are confirmed complete — converting the implicit ordering to a documented sequenced step.
findings.finding: The deploy ordering between this voter code going live and d251's provisioned artifacts (LLM-Review label, submit requirement, service account) is not explicitly stated as a sequenced step. While the default `enabled=false` safely prevents vote attempts before activation, the plan does not document an explicit instruction that `enabled=true` must only be set AFTER d251 provisioning is confirmed complete — leaving an implicit ordering that could be violated by an operator unfamiliar with the dependency.
findings.location: Dependencies section / Staged opt-in + rollback section
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Staged opt-in + rollback' section, add a bullet: '**Activation prerequisite:** set `enabled=true` only after confirming d251's LLM-Review label, submit requirement, copyCondition, and service-account are provisioned in the target Gerrit instance. The default `enabled=false` is safe to deploy at any time; activation is the sequenced step.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly that the patchset-created handler is idempotent under duplicate/at-least-once event delivery (Gerrit label voting is last-write-wins; robot comments should be posted at most once per patchset, e.g. by using Gerrit's robot-comment `robotId`+`fixId` dedup key or a guard check), and add a stub test asserting that invoking the handler twice for the same patchset results in exactly one robot comment and one label vote.
findings.finding: The plan does not state that the patchset-created handler is idempotent under at-least-once event delivery. If the webhook or event bus retries a patchset-created notification (network blip, consumer restart), two handler invocations may race and both attempt to vote on the same patchset. Gerrit label votes are naturally last-write-wins (PUT semantics), making duplicate votes benign, but this property is never stated or tested — leaving an implementer free to add non-idempotent side effects (e.g., appending duplicate robot comments).
findings.location: REST vote failure behavior section + AC line 6 (stub-driven test)
findings.suggested_fix: Gerrit's robot-comment API accepts a `robotId` field; posting a robot comment with the same `robotId` on the same patchset is idempotent (Gerrit deduplicates). Specify that the handler sets `robotId = f'LLM-Review:{change_id}:{patchset_number}'` and rely on Gerrit's dedup. Document this in the REST client spec and assert it in the stub test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the patchset-created handler design whether a per-(change_id, revision) single-flight guard is inherited from d251's receiver, re-implemented in `src/rebar/gerrit/`, or explicitly accepted as out-of-scope with rationale (e.g., 'webhook delivery is serialized; concurrent patchset events are impossible in this deployment topology').
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new patchset-created handler in `src/rebar/gerrit/` but does not specify whether concurrent invocations for the same `(change_id, revision)` are guarded against a double-review / double-vote race. d251 explicitly solved this with a per-(change,revision) single-flight lock (per d251's Round-11 remediation: 'a per-(change,revision) SINGLE-FLIGHT lock guards the review+vote critical section so a webhook retry or backfill firing mid-review cannot spawn a second review'). WS6-a is stub-testable offline but the plan does not state whether this guard is inherited from d251, re-implemented here, or intentionally omitted (e.g., because stub-Gerrit tests won't exercise it). Without explicit treatment, the implementer may omit it, causing double LLM gate runs and potentially conflicting vote POSTs.
findings.location: AC3 (patchset-created handler); 'REST vote failure behavior' section

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
